text: Writing clean code is like writing a clear sentence. The most important goal is readability, which means anyone can understand your code quickly. Use proper naming conventions: choose descriptive variable names like totalPrice instead of x, and verb-based function names like calculateTotal(). Keep functions short and focused on one task. This makes your code easier to test and reuse. Finally, remember that clean code improves maintainability—future developers (including yourself) will thank you when they need to update or fix the program. A little extra effort now saves hours of confusion later.
